nodes.name: garden_plan.yaml
nodes.source:
# 2026 planting plan for Spring Gardens
# Maintained by Maria Alvarez

garden: Spring Gardens
year: 2026
head_gardener: Maria Alvarez

beds:
  - name: South Wall
    length_m: 18
    flowers:
      - rose: New Dawn
    notes: Climbing roses trained on horizontal wires.

  - name: Centre Court
    length_m: 12
    flowers:
      - rose: Crimson Glory
      - rose: Iceberg
    notes: Bush roses interplanted with garlic and chives.

  - name: Heritage Border
    length_m: 24
    flowers:
      - rose: Lady of Shalott
      - tulip: Queen of Night
      - tulip: Spring Green
      - sunflower: Velvet Queen
      - sunflower: Teddy Bear
      - daisy: Pomponette
    notes: Mixed border for year-round interest.

  - name: Front Walk
    length_m: 30
    flowers:
      - tulip: Apricot Beauty
      - tulip: Princess Irene
      - sunflower: Russian Mammoth
      - sunflower: Lemon Queen
    notes: Tulips in spring, sunflowers replace them in June.

related_documents:
  - README.md
  - roses.md
  - tulips.md
  - sunflowers.txt
  - care_guide.md
  - catalog.json
  - varieties.csv
  - info.html

nodes.name: sunflowers.txt
nodes.source:
Sunflowers at Spring Gardens
============================

Sunflowers are the giants of Spring Gardens. Maria Alvarez plants them
in the Front Walk every June, where they replace the spent tulips and
provide a wall of yellow until late September.

Featured varieties
------------------

- Russian Mammoth — the classic yellow giant, up to 3.5 metres tall.
- Velvet Queen   — a deep crimson-red sunflower for the Heritage
                   Border.
- Lemon Queen    — a soft pale-yellow multi-headed variety.
- Teddy Bear     — a fluffy dwarf sunflower (60 cm) for the front of
                   the border.

See catalog.json for the full list, varieties.csv for a quick table,
and garden_plan.yaml for where each sunflower lives.

Care notes
----------

Sunflowers are the easiest crop in Spring Gardens. Maria's routine is:

  1. Sow seeds direct in late May once the soil is warm.
  2. Water deeply once a week — sunflowers are drought tolerant once
     established.
  3. Stake the tall varieties (Russian Mammoth, Velvet Queen) once
     they reach 1 metre.

Companion planting
------------------

Sunflowers are kept on the opposite side of the garden from the roses
because they shade them out. They are interplanted with squash and
sweetcorn (a "three sisters" planting) and bordered with daisies.

Photograph: see sunflower_photo.jpeg.

nodes.name: info.html
nodes.source:
Spring Gardens

Welcome to Spring Gardens, the four-acre demonstration garden tended by Maria Alvarez. This leaflet links to everything you need to plan your visit.

Featured flowers

  • Roses — including New Dawn, Crimson Glory, Iceberg, and Lady of Shalott.
  • Tulips — including Queen of Night, Apricot Beauty, and Spring Green.
  • Sunflowers — including Russian Mammoth and Velvet Queen.
  • Daisies — Pomponette and Tasso Pink, lining every border.

Plan your visit

See the 2026 garden plan for the bed layout, the varieties table for a quick bloom-time reference, and the catalog for botanical names. The full care guide is also published here.

Photographs

Maria Alvarez, head gardener — Spring Gardens, 2026.
